task: Write a Python function truncate_words(s, n) returning the first n whitespace-separated words of the string joined by single spaces, so runs of whitespace collapse. When n is zero or negative return ''. When the string has fewer than n words return them all. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code meets all requirements, including handling the edge case where n is zero or negative.
trace: I approached this problem by first splitting the input string `s` into a list of words using the `split()` method. Then, I checked if the number of provided words (n) is zero or negative, in which case I returned an empty string. If the length of the word list was less than n, I joined all the words together with single spaces and returned that. Finally, when there were more than n words, I used slice notation to return only the first n words joined by a single space. The edge case handled here is when the provided number of words (n) exceeds the total number of words in the input string.
